key_insight: WeldMamba predicts the future geometry of keyhole, wire, and molten pool regions from past images plus process/sensor signals to enable lookahead weld-pool segmentation under control delay.
engineering_relevance: This provides a path to more reliable closed-loop laser wire-feed welding by compensating for sensing and actuation latency with physics-guided future state prediction.
method: Uses coaxial grayscale image history with welding parameters and wire-state electrical signals as multimodal inputs.
Applies a spatiotemporal state-space model with process/sensor-conditioned normalization and horizon-conditioned latent prediction.
Adds motion-aware decoding and auxiliary losses (SDF, temporal consistency, distillation, keyhole supervision) to preserve geometry.
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead.
watch_item: Performance may depend on accurate signal alignment and the specific welding setup, so generalization to other materials, torch geometries, or sensing configurations should be validated.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
